mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and PKC signaling kinases to reduce proliferation in FLT3-driven AML
additional_notes: Approved in FLT3+ AML; described as PKC/FLT3 multi-kinase inhibitor

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinases and receptor tyrosine kinases including VEGFR and FLT3
additional_notes: Used in oncology; studied in FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases including FLT3
additional_notes: Approved for RCC/GIST; noted as multi-kinase including FLT3

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases and ABL to suppress downstream proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Reported activity in subsets of AML; often discussed in combinations

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway to suppress downstream signaling
additional_notes: Listed as MEK1/2 inhibitor; downstream pathway suppression in AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits EGFR tyrosine kinase; noted for off-target/repurposing activity in some AML settings
additional_notes: EGFR inhibitor; text notes off-target/repurposing activity in AML

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin acetylation and gene expression
additional_notes: Alternative name: SAHA; approved for CTCL; studied in AML

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to modulate transcription and promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in multiple myeloma

mechanism: At higher concentrations inhibits HDACs, affecting transcriptional programs and differentiation
additional_notes: Long clinical history; explicitly noted HDAC inhibitor at higher exposures

mechanism: Incorporates into nucleic acids and inhibits DNA methylation to reactivate silenced genes
additional_notes: Approved for MDS/AML

mechanism: Nucleoside analog that inhibits DNA methyltransferases, reducing DNA methylation
additional_notes: Approved for MDS/AML

mechanism: Selectively inhibits BCL-2 to trigger apoptosis in susceptible AML cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; noted that many AML lines respond

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 family proteins (BCL-2 and BCL-XL) to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Alternative name: ABT-263; described as clinical oncology drug

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome, disrupting protein degradation and inducing proteotoxic stress/apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in multiple myeloma

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, causing accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in multiple myeloma

category: Metabolic modulator (mitochondrial complex I / AMPK axis)
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and influences AMPK-related metabolic signaling
additional_notes: Referenced in AML metabolism studies

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial electron transport to impair cellular energy metabolism
additional_notes: Anticancer repurposing literature mentioned

mechanism: Inhibits ALDH; noted with cuproptosis-related hypotheses in AML repurposing
additional_notes: Text mentions ALDH inhibition and cuproptosis-related hypotheses

mechanism: Induces oxidative/stress responses (ROS-related) with reported activity in hematologic cancers
additional_notes: Antimalarial; hematologic cancer reports noted

mechanism: Accumulates in lysosomes and inhibits autophagy/lysosomal function, often used for combination rationale
additional_notes: Two related drugs listed together: chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine; described as lysosomotropic autophagy inhibitors
